posts.author: TheEconomist

posts.text:
Retail investors are piling into energy stocks:

Individual investor net buying of the US Energy Sector ETF, $XLE, jumped +427% between Friday and Monday.

In the first 30 minutes of Monday's session, retail net turnover exceeded $4 million, marking a +300% surge from Friday and +700% since Wednesday.

As a result, retail investor purchases have surpassed the combined demand of the previous 3 full trading days.

Buying interest in technology stocks was also robust, with Nvidia, $NVDA, and Palantir, $PLTR, seeing strong inflows.

$PLTR inflows ranked in the top 25% of all days over the last month.

Retail risk appetite remains incredibly strong.

posts.text:
$hg $hgraf has many patents.  Not just for producing fractal graphene aggregates but working with them to make the worlds next generation of superior unbeatable products.  Graphene ink, nano spheres, actuators etc. 

I’m not at all to say that I see a path for it to become one of the most valued companies in the world.  Don’t like criminals fool you with lies. Study the science. 

Science is based on fundamental truths.  Understand them and prosper. Forget the jargon… you can understand quantum theory.  Don’t let the arrogant among us tell you otherwise.  Don’t let them insult you and call you low iq and tell you it’s too complicated. And to move on.  Instead. Dig deep 
Be brave. Understand what so many are simply too lazy or lazy the intellectual curiosity to understand. 

Understanding the quantum age we are entering is key.  

The Quantum age as arrived!  

And Hydrograph’s fractal graphene is at the center of it.  As a shareholder you get a front row seat. 

(Watch everyone coin this term like crazy now)
